dmesg_evidence: NVRM: krcWatchdog_IMPL: RC watchdog: GPU is probably locked! (repeating every ~8s)
NVRM: nvAssertFailedNoLog: Assertion failed: (status == NV_OK) || (status == NV_ERR_GPU_IN_FULLCHIP_RESET) @ gpu_user_shared_data.c:373
root_cause_description: nvidia-persistenced OOM-killed 2 days ago (B) caused persistence mode loss; GPU subsequently entered RC watchdog FULLCHIP_RESET locked state. GPU is primary display device held by gnome-shell (D), so hot-reset via nvidia-smi blocked. Requires full system reboot or display session teardown to clear lock.
next_steps_for_user: 1. (Recommended) Reboot ubu host: `sudo reboot` — clears GPU FULLCHIP_RESET lock cleanly.

2. (Alternative, risky) Drop to multi-user.target to release gnome-shell, then rmmod/modprobe nvidia modules, then retry gpu-reset: `sudo systemctl isolate multi-user.target && sudo rmmod nvidia_uvm nvidia_drm nvidia_modeset nvidia && sudo modprobe nvidia && sudo systemctl isolate graphical.target` — will kill user desktop.
3. (Preventive) Add a systemd override so nvidia-persistenced auto-restarts on OOM: `sudo systemctl edit nvidia-persistenced` → add [Service] Restart=on-failure / RestartSec=10s.
4. After recovery, re-run CLM r6 GPU smoke to confirm VRAM access and compute path.
verdict_rationale: Service layer recovered (nvidia-persistenced restarted, persistence mode enabled) but GPU compute path remains locked in FULLCHIP_RESET state that is not recoverable without reboot due to primary-GPU constraint. Hardware itself is healthy (lspci sees it, modules load, VRAM total reports correctly).
